summary: Phase 5 Lead 360° Intelligence backend: 24/24 new tests passed. Combined with prior regression suites (Phase 4 multi-industry + Phase 3 support tickets) -> 60/60 GREEN. All Phase 5 features verified: duplicate hard-block (409 on mobile/email with existing_lead payload), live check-duplicate endpoint, lead timeline auto-logging (lead_created/status_changed/lead_lost/admission_recorded/assigned/transferred/followup_added), lead transfer RBAC (manager/admin allowed, counselor & telecaller 403, invalid assignee 400), voice upload to Cloudinary with 3MB/180s/mime gates, follow-ups persist voice fields and log timeline, admissions trigger admission_recorded timeline event. Tenant isolation verified (super_admin in different org gets 404 on demo-org lead timeline).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 2280 lines — strongly recommend splitting into routers (auth.py, leads.py, followups.py, admissions.py, uploads.py, organization.py, support.py). The 'I will refactor later' technical debt is now blocking readability.

Voice upload (POST /api/uploads/voice-recording) reads the entire file into memory before size check. For a 3 MB cap this is acceptable, but tighten by streaming or pre-checking Content-Length header to avoid DoS via partial uploads that exceed cap.

POST /api/leads/{id}/transfer: when previous_assignee_id is set but the user doc has been deleted, db.users.find_one returns None and the code uses 'previous_assignee.get("name") if previous_assignee else None' which is safe — BUT the bare ObjectId(previous_assignee_id) call will raise InvalidId if previous_assignee_id is not a 24-char hex (e.g., legacy string). Wrap in try/except or validate before ObjectId().

POST /api/leads/{id}/transfer accepts new_assignee_id of any string but immediately passes to ObjectId(). Invalid (non-ObjectId) input will return 500 instead of 400. Add Pydantic field validation or try/except around ObjectId(new_assignee_id).

GET /api/leads/{id}/timeline & PUT /api/leads/{id} both call ObjectId(lead_id) on a path param without try/except — a malformed lead_id raises 500 instead of 422/404. Recommend a dependency that validates 24-char hex.

log_lead_event accepts lead_id as str|ObjectId and converts; good. But timeline payloads embed raw user IDs (from/to assigned_to) without resolved names for the 'assigned' event from PUT /leads/{id} — consider resolving user.name like transfer does, for UI display without N+1 lookups.

Duplicate check in POST /api/leads uses {'$or': [{mobile}, {email}]} but the matched_on attribution string assumes mobile match if mobile equals — correct for current path. However if email-only payload (no mobile sent) ever occurs, mobile is required by LeadCreate so this is moot; consider documenting.

Voice upload returns duration from Cloudinary (probe) but if cloudinary returns 0/None for short clips, falls back to client-supplied duration which is unvalidated against actual file. Client could spoof duration=10 on a 200s file; trust the server-side probe when available.
test_credentials: Used credentials from /app/memory/test_credentials.md (super_admin admin@educationcrm.com, orgadmin@demo.com, manager@demo.com, counselor@demo.com, telecaller@demo.com — all with their respective Demo@123/Admin@123 passwords).
seed_data_creation: All test leads/admissions/followups created in the demo org with TEST_ prefixed names and unique 9-digit mobiles / @phaseatests.com emails. Session-scoped autouse fixture deletes every created lead via DELETE /api/leads/{id} after test session completes. Voice recordings are uploaded to Cloudinary under folder leadtrak/voice/{demo_org_id} — small (1s WAV silence) and remain in Cloudinary (not deleted by tests).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 5 backend is fully green. test_lead_360.py is the canonical regression for lead timeline / transfer / duplicate-block / voice upload / admission timeline. Notes: (1) Voice upload requires a REAL audio file to satisfy Cloudinary — generate minimal WAV via stdlib `wave` module, do NOT send random bytes (Cloudinary rejects with 500). (2) Dashboard funnel education-industry final stage label is 'Enrolled' (not 'Admission Done'). (3) Demo org IDs and lead_id counter continue incrementing across runs — tests use ObjectId-shaped fake IDs (24 hex chars) to test 400 on transfer; non-hex strings would currently 500 (see code-review comment).
rca of the issue: No defects found in Phase 5 implementation. Two test-script-only fixes were needed during authoring: (a) voice upload needed a real WAV (Cloudinary rejected synthetic bytes with 'Unsupported video format or file'); (b) education-industry funnel final stage label is 'Enrolled' (not 'Admission Done'/'Won') — adjusted assertion to accept the industry-mapped label set.
